evals.prompt: Set the priority for this task: 'File quarterly BAS'. It has a deadline in 4 days. Currently no priority set. The task is in the Finance area.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P1 with a specific due date within the next 1-2 days

assertions.name: proposes_p1
assertions.description: Should propose P1 for a task with a deadline in 4 days

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific day (e.g. Monday, Tuesday, or a specific date) as the due date
assertions.description: P1 tasks should always get a specific due date

assertions.name: no_extended_questioning
assertions.expected: Makes a direct proposal without asking multiple clarifying questions
assertions.description: Priority should be obvious here — no need to ask what happens if it's not done

evals.prompt: Set the priority for this task: 'Align budget to current lifestyle'. Description: 'Review actual spending from the last few months. Create or update a budget that reflects current income, expenses, and savings goals.' No deadline. Currently no priority. It's in a Finance project.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P2 with a due date suggestion this week or next

assertions.name: proposes_p2
assertions.description: Important financial task with no deadline should be P2

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Suggests a specific day to work on this task
assertions.description: P2 tasks should usually get a due date

assertions.name: not_p1
assertions.description: No deadline or urgency means this shouldn't be P1

evals.prompt: Set the priority for this task: 'Clean up iCloud folder'. No description. No deadline. No one waiting. It's in Personal.
evals.expected_output: Should propose P3 or P4 with no due date

assertions.name: proposes_p3_or_p4
assertions.description: Low-stakes cleanup task should be P3 or P4

assertions.name: no_due_date
assertions.expected: Does not propose or suggest setting a due date
assertions.description: P3/P4 tasks should not get due dates

assertions.name: keeps_it_brief
assertions.expected: Response is short — a quick proposal, not a lengthy analysis
assertions.description: Obvious low-priority tasks should be fast

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Research what types of insurance are required for consultants in Australia'. Currently P3, no due date. Context: the user is signing their first consulting client next week and needs insurance before starting work.
evals.expected_output: Should propose bumping to P1 or P2 with a specific due date

assertions.name: proposes_bump
assertions.expected: Proposes changing priority from P3 to P1 or P2
assertions.description: Changed context (signing client next week) makes this urgent

assertions.name: proposes_due_date
assertions.expected: Proposes a specific day as due date
assertions.description: Bumped to P1/P2 means it needs a date

assertions.name: references_context_change
assertions.expected: Mentions the client signing or starting work as the reason for the bump
assertions.description: Should explain why the priority changed

evals.prompt: Re-evaluate the priority for this task: 'Message Brad Lorge to arrange a catchup'. Currently P4, no due date. No new context — just a social catchup with a friend.
evals.expected_output: Should confirm P4 is correct, no date, move on quickly

assertions.name: confirms_p4
assertions.expected: Confirms P4 is the right priority or says it looks fine as-is
assertions.description: No reason to change priority on a social catchup

assertions.name: no_due_date
assertions.expected: Does not propose setting a due date
assertions.description: P4 tasks should never get dates

assertions.name: moves_on_quickly
assertions.expected: Response is brief — confirms and moves on, no unnecessary analysis
assertions.description: When priority is obviously fine, don't dwell on it
